biases_detected: shell_tool_preference_violations:RECURRING:STABLE — db668075-2: cat package.json|head-80 in compound Bash instead of Read (success=true but tool preference violation); ls-la|head-40 compound Bash success=false before decomposition. db668075-18: grep -qE in polling for-loop instead of rg (success=true). Violations in ~3 of 8 units (~37%); within the 30-44% STABLE band of all prior cycles. No improvement trajectory observed across full calibration history spanning six sessions.

compound_command_overreach:RECURRING:STABLE — db668075-2: ls-la+echo+ls next.config compound Bash success=false before decomposition. db668075-7: large compound (kill-USR1+curl+cat-heredoc+node) success=false before Write-fallback adopted. Decompose-only-after-failure sequence structurally identical across all prior cycles. No pre-decomposition behavior observed in any confirmed instance across full calibration history.

rm_bash_instead_of_trash:RECURRING — db668075-18: rm -rf .next executed in Bash, blocked by safe-delete hook (success=false); recovered correctly with trash .next in subsequent command. Third confirmed instance. Hook enforcement effective; root-cause prevention (pre-selection discipline) absent. Consistent with prior cycle reading.

re_edit_thrash:MARGINAL_IMPROVEMENT_CONFIRMING — db668075-21: Edit-fail on package.json → Read → Edit-succeed; Edit-fail on .gitignore → Read → Edit-succeed. Second consecutive cycle showing Read-before-retry behavior within edit-thrash-adjacent units rather than hook-bypass as exit. Four externally-justified consecutive Edits on playwright.perf.config.ts driven by real test-run feedback, not blind retry. Improvement trend appears consolidating; no hook bypass observed this cycle.

bash_glob_expansion_error:NEW — db668075-26: rg command with shell ** glob (rg -n 'pattern' node_modules/@sentry/nextjs/build/**/*.js) success=false; macOS bash 3.2 does not expand ** without globstar option. Recovered by removing glob in subsequent command. Single instance; not seen in prior calibration history. Sub-variant of shell scripting error class.
recommendations: shell_tool_preference_violations and compound_command_overreach are both STABLE across six consecutive cycles with no improvement trend; deprioritize new recommendations for these — enforcement hooks are the only effective signal and they already fire. Further advisory text has no measurable effect.
re_edit_thrash trajectory is positive (Read-before-retry confirmed two consecutive cycles, no hook-bypass observed); deprioritize this recommendation until regression appears.

bash_glob_expansion_error is new and single-instance; watch for recurrence before recommending mitigation. If it recurs, recommend pre-flight rg calls use explicit path enumeration (rg -l pattern dir/) rather than shell globs.
